checklist.claim: the new #366/#356 pin literal occurs exactly once in skills/implement/SKILL.md and grep -vF removes exactly one line
no path in the Stall backstop step writes --status Complete, so 'never drives a run to Complete' is true
checklist.evidence: only --status Failed appears in the step

checklist.claim: python3 exits 2 on an unopenable/unreadable script, colliding with cmd_id's rc-2 contract
checklist.evidence: reproduced: exit=2 with 'can't open file [Errno 2]' and [Errno 13]; cmd_id's sys.exit(2) is silent

phase3_findings.description: flip-review-progress-failed.sh treated ANY rc 2 from python3 as cmd_id's clean 'no match'. python3 also exits 2 when it cannot open the script (partial vendor copy / mode-000), so a read that never happened was reported as 'comment-absent' — falsifying the helper's own diff-added header comment. Guard's accepted-input set wider than its consumer's contract (#62/#98 class).
skills/implement/SKILL.md:156 enumerates the canonical glyph set as (🚀/🎉/👎); workpad.py now prepends 💥 too. Unpinned.
skills/retrospective/SKILL.md:92 enumerates workpad_final_status as Complete/Blocked/interim; this PR wires Failed into that exact consumer. Unpinned.
The interim->auth-failure reclassification path (a genuine interim whose attempt-count read fails) does not flip, leaving the workpad frozen. Honestly documented; deliberate.
no findings; verified the iter-2 fix's SKILL.md/overview/pin comments are all true against HEAD

fix_decisions.claim_text: rc-2 collapse misreports a failed read as comment-absent
fix_decisions.evidence: Shape 3 flagged (single-source from silent-failure-hunter, an empirical over-grader). Re-affirmed at Important on observable grounds, and it is ALSO a self-contradicting diff (corroboration-independent): the helper's diff-ADDED header comment states 'an operator is never told the comment does not exist when the read merely failed', which I reproduced as FALSE against HEAD. Fail direction: the flip is a no-op either way (safe), but the breadcrumb actively misdirects an operator debugging a frozen comment, and the guard's accepted-input set is wider than cmd_id's contract. Not fail-closed in the diagnostic dimension; the suite did not catch it RED (no coverage existed). The 4.1.5 comment-only cap does not apply (this is executable control flow, and the comment involved was diff-added).

Shape 3 flagged (single-source from pr-test-analyzer). Re-affirmed: demonstrably false against HEAD (workpad.py's _STATUS_GLYPHS is a 4-tuple and _status_glyph prepends 💥). Operative agent-facing documentation of the very flag the backstop drives (--status Failed). Not inert prose; not suite-RED (unpinned before this fix).

Shape 3 flagged. Re-affirmed: false against HEAD, and the retrospective is the precise consumer this PR wires Failed into (FAILED-* fixtures -> cheap-gate). The Stage-A prompt would hand its LLM a value its own enumeration does not cover. Unpinned; not suite-RED.

Suggestion severity. Defensible and honestly documented: a flip via workpad.py update would hit the same failing gh API and no-op anyway; the docs enumerate exactly the four flipping arms and never claim this one flips, so it is not a documented falsehood.

fix_decisions.claim_text: the review skill's own `workpad.py id` rc-2 branch has the same interpreter-rc-2 hazard: a missing workpad.py would be read as 'first write' and post a duplicate progress comment
fix_decisions.evidence: skills/review/SKILL.md is NOT in this PR's diff (git diff --name-only over the merge-base shows it untouched), and this PR does not widen reliance on that branch. Genuine sibling of the fixed class — worth a follow-up issue, not an in-scope fix.

fix_decisions.claim_text: a scenario-5 comment describes the glyph strip set as (🚀/🎉/👎)
fix_decisions.evidence: Pre-existing comment, last touched in df6703e; not diff-added/modified, and >10 lines from any hunk this PR changes, so the widens-surface guard does not disqualify the skip. Cosmetic comment_drift about a fixture's own scenario.

fix_decisions.note: Step 3.5 gate suggestion: the unreadable-sibling test skipped silently under a mode-bit-ignoring uid; now emits a SKIP breadcrumb.
Step 3.5 gate suggestion: recorded the explicit---marker invariant the rc-2 stderr discriminator relies on.
